explanation: This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The atomic proposition "state_is_TRANSITION" checks for the current state being TRANSITION.  The atomic proposition "state_is_STANDBY" checks for the current state being STANDBY.  The atomic proposition "standby" checks if the pilot is in control. The property uses a Globally operator (G) to ensure this behavior holds at all times.  The property uses an implication (->) to define the condition for state change. The Finally operator (F) with bounded time ensures that the transition happens within a specific time frame and the Globally operator ensures the condition is checked indefinitely.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby). It uses template 14. bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION). bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot is in STANDBY state and the pilot is in control (state_is_STANDBY & standby). The formula ensures this transition happens within N_DURATION time units.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within 2 time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The chosen template accounts for the possibility of the transition not occurring and the system being in the TRANSITION state indefinitely.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will transition to the STANDBY state in the next time step if the pilot is in control (standby).  It uses the template that specifies a next state condition and is based on a previous state condition. The boolean expression bool_exp2 checks if the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, and bool_exp3 checks if the pilot is in control and the autopilot transitions to STANDBY in the next state. N_DURATION is set to 1 as the transition is expected to happen in the very next time step.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The formula uses the implication operator to express the conditional behavior.  If bool_exp2 (state_is_TRANSITION) is true, then bool_exp3 (state_is_STANDBY & standby) must become true within the specified time bound. The use of F ensures the eventuality, and the time bound N_DURATION adds a quantitative constraint.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The implication (->) ensures that if bool_exp2 (state_is_TRANSITION) is true, then within N_DURATION time units bool_exp3 (state_is_STANDBY & standby) must also be true. The G operator ensures this property holds globally (at every time instant).

This LTL property checks that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control.  The boolean expression bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION). The boolean expression bool_exp3 represents the condition that the pilot is in control (standby) and the autopilot is in the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY). The property uses the F (eventually) operator to check that the STANDBY state is reached within the specified time bound. The G (globally) operator ensures this behavior holds at all times.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The formula uses the implication operator (->) to capture the conditional nature of the transition, where bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION), and bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot is in the STANDBY state and the pilot is in control (state_is_STANDBY & standby). The G operator ensures that this implication holds globally (in all states), and the F operator with the bounded interval [0, N_DURATION] ensures that the transition occurs within the specified time limit. The N_DURATION parameter provides flexibility for specifying a reasonable time for the transition to complete. If the transition does not occur within this time, it indicates a potential fault.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The atomic proposition state_is_TRANSITION indicates the current state is TRANSITION, and standby indicates the pilot is in control. The implication ensures that the transition to STANDBY occurs only when standby is true.  The F operator ensures that the transition to STANDBY eventually occurs within the specified time bound.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby). The boolean expression bool_exp2 checks for the autopilot being in the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION). The boolean expression bool_exp3 checks if the pilot is in control (standby). The property uses the G (always) operator to ensure that this behavior holds globally. The F (eventually) operator with the interval [0,N_DURATION] guarantees the transition to STANDBY happens within the specified timeframe.

This LTL formula states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The formula uses the implication operator to express the conditional behavior.  If the pilot is in control, then the transition to the STANDBY state must occur. Otherwise, it is not required.  The G operator ensures this behavior is globally true across all states and time instances.

The property uses the F (eventually) temporal operator to describe the occurrence of the STANDBY state within a limited timeframe (N_DURATION).

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will transition to the STANDBY state in the next time step if the pilot is in control (standby). This captures the natural language requirement by specifying the transition condition and the next state.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units, provided that the pilot is in control (standby).  The formula uses the implication (->) to express the condition.  It iterates through time using the globally operator (G). The formula ensures that the transition happens within the specified time bound, preventing infinite loops in the TRANSITION state.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units, if the pilot is in control (standby).  G[0,N_DURATION] is used to ensure the transition happens in a bounded time. The formula uses implication (->) to express the condition that if the autopilot is in TRANSITION, then it will be in STANDBY after some time. G ensures this property holds globally throughout the system's operation.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units, if the pilot is in control (standby).  The implication (->) ensures the transition only happens under the condition that the pilot is in control. G means globally (always), X means next, and F[0, N_DURATION] means that something happens within N_DURATION time units.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within 2 time units if the pilot is in control (standby).  The G operator ensures that this is always true. The -> operator means implication. X means next state. F means eventually. G means globally. [0,N_DURATION] means within N_DURATION time units. The bool_exp2 (state_is_TRANSITION) checks if the autopilot is currently in TRANSITION mode. The bool_exp3 (state_is_STANDBY & standby) checks if the autopilot is in STANDBY mode and if the pilot is in control. The N_DURATION is set to 2 to limit the transition time.

The implication G(bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3) ensures that this transition happens globally, meaning that the condition must always hold. If the pilot is not in control, the condition is vacuously true.
